overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees approved increased park and recreation impact fees, passed ordinances regulating mobile food vendors and e-bikes, and discussed ongoing challenges with the farmers market's new Kennedy Park location. The board also adjourned the Board of Review to June 15, 2026 and approved various license renewals and committee appointments.

agenda.item: Board of Review called to order and adjourned to June 15, 2026
Board of Trustees meeting called to order and roll call
Public hearing on park and recreation impact fees (Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015)
Acknowledge reports from boards, committees, and commissions with chair updates
Ordinances including mobile food vendor, nuisances, e-bikes, and transportation funding resolution
Discussion on farmers market location at Kennedy Park

discussions.body: Staff presented proposed fee increases from $300 to $761 for single family units, $250 to $563 for duplex units, and $250 to $495 for multifamily units, based on the 2022 public facilities needs assessment. Jim Pitel spoke in public comment questioning the justification for the fees, stating the village lacks detailed data on actual park maintenance costs. Trustee Barb Clark supported the fees as justified given population growth. Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015 both passed unanimously.

Chief Finkey explained the ordinance creates a regional inspection agreement where Safer, Marshfield, Wisconsin Rapids, Stevens Point, Clover, and Riverside fire departments will recognize each other's food truck inspections. A sticker system will indicate which department inspected a truck, eliminating redundant inspections across municipalities. Ordinance 26-008 passed unanimously.

The board debated amendments to section 1.08 of the elected officials handbook. Several trustees wanted to remove specific language about health, safety, and welfare requirements for remote attendance, preferring broader discretion. Trustee Beck moved to amend by striking sentences requiring specific justifications for remote attendance. The amendment passed 5-2 with Trustees Hermling and Lob Sorrell opposed. The original motion as amended also passed 5-2 with the same dissenting votes.

President appointed Stephanie Daniels (currently on Finance and HR) and Golden Mua to the CLIPS committee. Both appointments were approved unanimously. The board discussed having committee applications shared in packets going forward. Parks, Finance/HR, and CLIPS still have openings.

Board discussed challenges with the farmers market's new Kennedy Park location after losing their previous Camp Phillips/Schofield Avenue site to development. Trustee Lisa Beck raised safety concerns about parking on grass along Alderson Street. Renee explained the history: the market grew from 25 stalls at the old municipal building to 42 at Camp Phillips (where vehicles couldn't accompany vendors) to 53 spots at Kennedy Park where vendors can keep vehicles. Beck questioned costs but staff confirmed the market operates without loss through vendor fees and grants. No action was taken; discussion only.

During public comment, Jim Pitel questioned why the engineering technician position remains unfilled while the village pays outside consultants. Public Works Director Michael Dolan explained the position has been advertised multiple times but the specific skill set is scarce in central Wisconsin, noting even consulting firms struggle to find local candidates and pull from southeastern Wisconsin offices.

The board approved a secondhand article dealer and secondhand jewelry dealer license for Schofield Coin and Hobby LLC at 5703 Jamar Street. Chief Hunt explained the business deals in precious metals and gold with large dollar transactions. The motion was amended to require satisfactory background check completion and receipt of occupancy permit since the building is still under construction. Approved unanimously as amended.

publicComment: Jim Pitel of 52nd Street spoke twice. First, he criticized the village for spending $108,000 on outside engineering consultants while the engineering technician position remains unfilled, questioned why Lisa Beck was introduced at a Safer meeting before being officially appointed, complained about lack of Kennedy Park information in meeting packets, and criticized the administrator's accountability. During the impact fee public hearing, he questioned the justification for fee increases, stating the village lacks detailed data on actual park maintenance costs. No other public comments were offered.
actionItems: Park and recreation impact fees increased to $761 for single family, $563 for duplex, and $495 for multifamily units effective immediately
Board of Review adjourned to June 15, 2026
Mobile food vendor regional inspection agreement ordinance adopted
Remote meeting attendance policy amended to remove specific justification requirements
Stephanie Daniels and Golden Mua appointed to CLIPS committee
Secondhand dealer license approved for Schofield Coin and Hobby LLC pending background check and occupancy permit
Comprehensive Plan steering committee first meeting scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 6:00 PM
Staff to prepare intergovernmental service agreement for Ringle building inspection assistance
Weston Avenue speed limit study to be completed and brought back to board
Committee and commission applications to be included in board packets going forward
title: Board of Trustees
